Will Starlink ever be faster than fiber? 
 
No, Starlink will likely never be faster than fiber optic internet.  
Fiber transmits data using light through glass, moving nearly at the speed of
posts.body:
$SPCX  
Will Starlink ever be faster than fiber? 
 
No, Starlink will likely never be faster than fiber optic internet.  
Fiber transmits data using light through glass, moving nearly at the speed of light. It routinely provides multi-gigabit speeds (1 Gbps to 10 Gbps) and near-zero lag (5-10 ms latency). By contrast, Starlink averages 100–250 Mbps and 20–40 ms latency, heavily constrained by space-to-ground distances and atmospheric factors.

Simple loop: 
1) Buy the core — $VOO, $QQQ, plus a few strong single names. 
2) Let quality compound. 
3) Sell portfolio-secured puts when great names get cheap. 
4) Repeat. 
 
Not flashy, but it keeps you in the game and buys fear when others are forced to sell.

posts.body:
$IPST  
 
What makes IPST interesting: 
 
-It’s one of the first public companies trying a “crypto treasury” model around intellectual-property tokens instead of Bitcoin. 
-If Story Protocol adoption grows, speculative traders could pile into IPST similarly to how some traders treat Bitcoin treasury companies. 
-The company reportedly acquired tens of millions of $IP tokens through a large financing transaction and now holds substantial crypto assets on its balance sheet.

Who's gonna file the class action lawsuit?  
Rebranding & Shift: In late 2025, Heritage Distilling pivoted from a craft spirits business to a "digital asset treasury strategy," renaming itself IP Strategy (IPST) and focusing on the Story $IP token. 
Stock Performance: The company's stock experienced massive declines (roughly 85% in four months following its IPO) and fell below $1.00 per share, triggering delisting concerns in April 2025.

posts.body:
Packaging stocks moved higher Monday after Citi analyst Anthony Pettinari said Smurfit Westrock is raising U.S. corrugated boxboard prices by $50 per ton, effective June 1. 
Shares of Smurfit Westrock rose 1.7%, while International Paper and Packaging Corporation gained around 3% each, reversing earlier declines following the news.

posts.body:
Google announced plans to build a new data center in Michigan in partnership with DTE Energy, committing to enable 2.7 GW of new clean energy to support the local grid. 
The project, under evaluation in Van Buren Township, will add 24/7 clean power—including solar, advanced storage, and demand flexibility—through a Clean Capacity Acceleration Agreement, supporting the state’s transition away from coal while improving grid reliability.

posts.body:
$HBM Nobody is doing the math.

Gold = $4,130. Silver = $61.62. Copper = $6.20/lb.

Q1 byproduct credits from their gold + silver alone: ~$4.93/lb of copper.

C1 cash cost was already -$1.80/lb in Q1 when gold was $3,000.
Run it at today’s metals prices — you’re looking at C1 approaching -$3.29/lb.

That’s $9.49 of margin on every pound of $6.20 copper they sell.
124,000 tons of copper guidance this year = 273M lbs.

Do the math. The stock is at $23. The market is sleeping.

Trump, in his Truth Social post on Friday, demanded that Senate Republicans eliminate filibuster rules in order to pass the election bill and other GOP priorities.

The rules require most legislation to clear a 60-vote procedural threshold, rather than advance by a simple majority, which is 51 votes if all 100 senators are present $VOO

posts.body:
Stocks may face short-term pressure ahead of mega IPO, KBW says

Stocks could come under pressure in the run-up to a blockbuster initial public offering as investors sell existing holdings to raise cash for the deal, according to KBW.

Historically, the S&P 500 has fallen a median 1.3% in the two weeks before U.S. IPOs larger than $10 billion, excluding American depositary receipts, as investors rotate out of large-cap stocks to fund participation in the offering, KBW said in a note.

posts.body:
Stock market and economy diverge

The S&P 500 U.S. stock index rose nearly 10% in the first half of 2026. The Dow Jones Industrial Average, another stock index, climbed almost 9% over the same period — its best first-half performance since 2021.

Those gains follow a string of blockbuster years for U.S. stocks: The S&P 500 rallied 24% in 2023, 23% in 2024 and 16% in 2025 — the second best three-year win streak since 2000.

$RKLB is working on one of the more interesting innovations in reusable rocket technology. 
 
Rocket Lab’s “Hungry Hippo” fairing concept could change how launch economics work by keeping the nose con
posts.body:
$RKLB is working on one of the more interesting innovations in reusable rocket technology. 
 
Rocket Lab’s “Hungry Hippo” fairing concept could change how launch economics work by keeping the nose cone attached to the booster instead of losing a major piece of hardware after each mission. 
 
If successful, this approach could reduce recovery complexity, lower costs, and improve the efficiency of future launches. 
 
The space industry is moving from experimental technology toward scalable infrast

Dow on track for break longest winning streak since 2024

Thursday’s rally failed to pull the Dow
 into the green for the week, meaning it could break a winning streak.

The 30-stock index added 0.3% in midday trading, but was still down nearly 1% on the week. If that holds, it would snap a four-week positive streak — the longest since late 2024.

Sherwin-Williams
 and Home Depot

MU.name: Micron Technology
MU.blurb: Micron is seen as undervalued with a low P/E and massive U.S. investment, but mention volume has plummeted.

SPY.name: SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust
SPY.blurb: Sentiment is mixed, with some blaming political events for market moves and others focusing on crude oil.

MSFT.name: Microsoft
MSFT.blurb: Bullish on AI backlog and long-term growth, with some buying the dip around $382.

SPCX.name: SpaceX
SPCX.blurb: SpaceX is a hot topic with strong bullish sentiment, though some messages are political.

QQQ.name: Invesco QQQ ETF
QQQ.blurb: Mixed sentiment, with some bullish on tech but others cautious on the overall market.

NVDA.name: NVIDIA
NVDA.blurb: Technical breakout expected, with many seeing it as undervalued and heading to $300+.

NFLX.name: Netflix
NFLX.blurb: Bullish ahead of earnings, with calls for a big move and price targets above $90.

IP.name: International Paper
IP.blurb: Extremely bullish on spin-off IPST and low float squeeze potential, with zero bearish tags.

DTE.name: DTE Energy
DTE.blurb: Bullish on growth and income, with no bearish sentiment in sampled messages.

HBM.name: Hudbay Minerals
HBM.blurb: Extremely bullish on copper demand from AI and power grids, with zero bearish tags.

VOO.name: Vanguard S&P 500 ETF
VOO.blurb: Strongly bullish, with one message calling it a consistent winner against market timers.

RKLB.name: Rocket Lab USA
RKLB.blurb: Bullish on Neutron rocket and upcoming bounce, but some advise buying at lower prices.

LIT.X.blurb: Discussed as a deflationary crypto token, with some seeing it as free money.

HD.blurb: Mixed, with some bullish on housing if yields fall, but others critical of competitors.

HBAR.X.blurb: Mixed sentiment; some frustrated by price action, others bullish on technology.

sentiment.reasoning: The overall mood is cautiously bullish, driven by strong bullish sentiment on several individual stocks like IP, HBM, and DTE, which have zero bearish tags in Stocktwits. However, broad market ETFs like SPY and QQQ show mixed sentiment, and mention counts have dropped sharply from 24 hours ago, suggesting some loss of enthusiasm.

themes.title: Semiconductor and AI Focus
themes.explanation: Tickers like MU, NVDA, and AMD are heavily discussed, with MU seeing a massive drop in mentions but still top-ranked. The community is focused on AI infrastructure build-out and semiconductor cycles, with bullish views on NVDA's breakout and MU's low valuation.

themes.title: Space and Defense Interest
themes.explanation: SPCX (SpaceX) and RKLB (Rocket Lab) are gaining attention, with SPCX jumping in rank. Bullish sentiment on SPCX is strong, and discussions highlight space as a growth sector, though some skepticism exists about valuation.

themes.title: Commodities and Energy
themes.explanation: HBM (copper) and DTE (energy) show extreme bullish sentiment with zero bearish tags. The community ties copper to AI and power grid demand, while DTE is seen as a growth and income play. This reflects a thematic shift toward real assets.

themes.title: Index ETF Divergence
themes.explanation: SPY, QQQ, and VOO are widely mentioned but with mixed sentiment. While VOO has strong bullish tags, SPY and QQQ are more balanced or bearish. This suggests uncertainty about broad market direction despite individual stock optimism.

themes.title: Crypto and Blockchain
themes.explanation: Crypto tickers like BTC-USD, ETH-USD, and HBAR.X appear in trending and sentiment data. HBAR.X has mixed sentiment, with some bullish on its technology but others frustrated by price action. The crypto discussion is separate from equities but still relevant.

tickers_to_watch.reason: MU has the most mentions despite a huge drop from 523 to 72. Stocktwits sentiment is strongly bullish (17 bullish vs 2 bearish). The community sees it as undervalued with a forward P/E of 7 and massive U.S. investment plans. Price could move on earnings or semiconductor news.

IP has 28 mentions, up from 15, and a perfect bullish sentiment score (25 bullish, 0 bearish). Discussions focus on its spin-off IPST and a low float squeeze potential. The stock is gaining attention for its digital asset rebrand and high revenue relative to market cap.

HBM has 24 mentions (up from 15) and extremely bullish sentiment (37 bullish, 0 bearish). The community ties it to copper demand from AI and power grids. With no bearish tags, it's a strong consensus pick, but investors should watch for copper price moves.

NFLX mentions rose from 58 to 31, and sentiment is bullish (16 bullish vs 8 bearish). The community expects a big move after earnings, with some predicting $90+ gains. Earnings this week could be a catalyst, but the stock is volatile.

SPCX (SpaceX) jumped from rank 8 to 4 with 53 mentions. Stocktwits sentiment is strongly bullish (20 bullish vs 6 bearish). The community is excited about space, but some messages are political. As a private company, it's not directly investable, but it influences sentiment on space ETFs.

risk_factors.title: Mention Volume Decline
risk_factors.explanation: Most top tickers saw a sharp drop in mentions from 24 hours ago, e.g., MU from 523 to 72, SPY from 418 to 60. This could indicate waning interest or a shift in focus. Lower engagement may lead to less price momentum.

risk_factors.title: Geopolitical Tensions
risk_factors.explanation: Several messages reference Iran and Israel, with some blaming political events for market moves. Geopolitical risks can cause sudden volatility, especially in oil and defense stocks, and may disrupt broader market sentiment.

risk_factors.title: Overconcentration in AI/Semiconductors
risk_factors.explanation: Many top tickers are in tech and semiconductors (MU, NVDA, AMD, MSFT). If AI hype fades or earnings disappoint, these stocks could correct sharply. The community's bullishness may be overdone.

risk_factors.title: Crypto Volatility Spillover
risk_factors.explanation: Crypto tickers like BTC-USD and ETH-USD are trending, and some stock discussions mix crypto references. A crypto crash could impact sentiment on related stocks and ETFs, especially those with crypto exposure.

contrarian_views.title: Bearish on Broad Market ETFs
contrarian_views.explanation: While many are bullish on individual stocks, sentiment on SPY and QQQ is mixed or bearish. Some traders think the market is overvalued and due for a pullback, citing political noise and economic uncertainty. This caution contrasts with the stock-level optimism.

contrarian_views.title: Skepticism on Space Stocks
contrarian_views.explanation: Despite bullish sentiment on SPCX and RKLB, some messages advise buying at lower prices (e.g., RKLB at $75-80). This suggests that even bulls see current prices as high, and there is risk of a pullback if earnings don't justify valuations.

contrarian_views.title: Crypto Disillusionment
contrarian_views.explanation: For HBAR.X, one message says 'chart is so bad I’m wondering why I ever bought this thing.' This highlights frustration among some crypto holders, even as others tout its technology. The split shows that not all crypto projects have community confidence.

discussion_digest: The biggest story today is the dramatic drop in mentions for top tickers like MU (from 523 to 72) and SPY (from 418 to 60). Despite this, MU remains the most mentioned stock, and the community is still bullish on it, citing a forward P/E of just 7 and a $250B U.S. investment plan. However, the volume decline suggests that the earlier frenzy may be cooling off, and investors should watch if this is a temporary lull or a shift in focus.

Space and defense stocks are gaining traction, with SPCX jumping from rank 8 to 4 and RKLB holding steady. Bullish sentiment on SPCX is strong (20 bullish vs 6 bearish), and messages highlight space as a growth sector. Meanwhile, IP and HBM have perfect bullish sentiment scores (zero bearish tags), driven by spin-off speculation and copper demand from AI. These stocks are seeing increased attention and could be momentum plays.

There's a clear divide between bullish individual stocks and cautious broad market ETFs. While IP, HBM, and DTE have no bearish tags, SPY and QQQ have mixed or bearish sentiment. Some traders are skeptical of the overall market, citing geopolitical risks and political noise. This tension suggests that while certain sectors are hot, the broader market may be vulnerable to a correction if sentiment shifts.

overlooked_insights.title: MU $250B U.S. Investment Plan
overlooked_insights.insight: A user on Stocktwits claims Micron is committing $250 billion to U.S. investment and over 100,000 jobs. If accurate, this would represent a massive long-term capital deployment that mainstream coverage (which focuses on earnings and SK Hynix's IPO) has not picked up. The same user also notes a forward P/E of just 7, suggesting the stock is deeply undervalued relative to this growth trajectory.

However, there are concerns about the broader economy: one user notes slower foot traffic at HD and other retailers, and rising costs for grilling (up 13% YoY) are squeezing consumers. The housing sector is under pressure from rising 10-year yields due to geopolitical tensions, but some expect cooling inflation from lower oil prices to help. Overall, the sentiment is cautiously optimistic, with a focus on rate cuts and housing data. Investors should monitor macroeconomic indicators like CPI and PCE, as well as housing market trends.

HBAR.X.summary: Hedera Hashgraph (HBAR) is generating mixed but passionate chatter on Stocktwits, with a bullish tilt (17 bullish vs 6 bearish tags). Many investors are frustrated with the price action, calling the chart "so bad" and lamenting "years wasted." However, a vocal group sees the current low price as a buying opportunity, especially for newcomers who can buy at a 50% discount compared to early adopters. They emphasize HBAR's technological strengths: speed, security, eco-friendliness, the Governing Council, and real-world use cases. There's impatience for HBAR to hit $1, and large purchases (e.g., 55,834 and 100,000 tokens) suggest conviction. Some holders are holding through the pain, with comments like "getting clobbered! Still holding though!" The broader alt-season question is raised, linking HBAR to XRP and XLM. Overall, the community is divided between those who believe in the long-term utility and those frustrated by poor price performance.
